title: Date Filtering Options (Between Dates YTD, MTD)
excerpt:
Currently storing some really valuable data about leads (Date Created, Date Contacted, Date Seen, Date Closed) but the filtering on date fields only offers This week, This Month, Past Dates, then individual dates. 
Such …

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently storing some really valuable data about leads (Date Created, Date Contacted, Date Seen, Date Closed) but the filtering on date fields only offers This week, This Month, Past Dates, then individual dates.

Such a shame adding more options here would open so many doors to allowing us to create really insightful reporting on lead progress filtered by dates.

The most valuable addition would be a ‘between dates’ filter option. Also Year to Date and Month to Date would be really helpful.

We want to have a chart that shows how many leads were created, Contacted, closed each month but for a specific year, not all time (that looks too big). If you could filter dates on charts, and ideally dashboard widgets too you could create much more specific, powerful charts.

Please consider this!

posts.author: Miltime19 (Adam Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: Please! This is such a necessary feature. The between dates filter I would guess is the most needed feature that Monday does not yet have. Please Monday.com, we need this!

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on whether date filtering options will be added at some point soon? As soon as this is unlocked I will be able to filter my board and dashboard to see the data i actually need to see!

This all seems to have gone quiet and I’m concerned it will never happen. Surely it can’t be ignored much longer I’ve seen multiple requests for it all with a lot of support but nobody has actually confirmed whether or not this will be done and it’s not on the roadmap.

Please revisit this request you’ll get so much back from this update as it opens so many doors.

posts.author: MHardy (Mark Hardy)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a MUCH needed feature for us. We are a consulting company who uses a variety of date ranges for reporting and billing, ie QTD, last month only, From A to B

Can I vote for this multiple times?

posts.author: CWaxman (CaraWaxman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on this? I need to filter by quarters for forecasting purposes as we forecast on a quarterly basis and there is no way for me to easily get an accurate number!

If anyone has a workaround that would be much appreciated!

posts.author: kennyfulton (Kenny Fulton)
posts.cookedHtml: Can I also suggest a date filter for “Last X Days”? I’d like to be able to display a rolling list of items that were completed in the last 30 days but the date filtering options are extremely limited.

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any updates on if or when this might be looked at? I’ve seen lots of dashboard changes so I’d have thought better date filtering would be high on the list of desired updates.

When adding filters to dashboards you also need to have matching data for it to be an option. Eg. I want to setup a dashboard for August now in advance, but I have to add fake data into my board with dates set to August, just so it appears in when configuring the filters. Would be good if the filters just gave you date parameters regardless of whether or not there is data which matches that criteria.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: This is needed in order to set our views.

posts.author: SirScannell (Dennis Scannell)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news on this. I think this feature would be a great advantage

posts.author: JordanD (Jordan Di Conti)
posts.cookedHtml: This is available now if you are using the Board filter v2

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
where can I find this Board Filter V2? Does it work with Subtasks?

If not a big bump on this for the devs / community mangers.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: where can I find this Board Filter V2? Does it work with Subtasks?

posts.author: goldmandw (Dylan)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, much needed feature to help us forecast requirements

posts.author: sarahmcd (Sarah McDermott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has there been any response on better date filtering?

In the actual dashboard/table, I can filter by ‘This Month’ + ‘Last Month’ but in the Chart Widget Filter options, for Connected Board #1 it’s showing as:

  • This Week
  • This Month
  • Past Dates
  • individual dates

for Connected Board #2, it’s showing:

  • Past Dates
  • individual dates

What we are wanting is to only show the past 3 months, but it seems impossible to even show the current month

I’ve already voted / and liked this suggestion, but a rolling date filter for “Last X Days” would be so useful!

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml:
I created a workaround by adding a Days Since Submitted formula column to my board.

ROUND(DAYS(TODAY(),{Creation Log#Date})-(2*(WEEKNUM(TODAY())-WEEKNUM({Creation Log#Date}))),1)

I then applied a filter to the widget on my dashboard to only show items created in the past 30 days.

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be ideal if we could have saved Views/Filters on dashboards so our users could easily switch between different dates filters/time periods without having to apply the filter each time. Not an exhaustive list but here are common reporting periods I have used in the past that I think would be helpful:

  • Week-to-date/calendar week
  • Rolling week
  • Month-to-date/calendar month
  • Rolling calendar
  • Quarter-to-date/calendar quarter
  • Rolling quarter
  • Year-to-date/calendar year
  • Rolling year
  • Week-over-week
  • Month-over-month
  • Month to prior year month
  • Quarter-over-quarter
  • Quarter to prior year quarter
  • Year-over-year

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any development on this? Filtering Dashboards to view only a certain time period would be really great.

posts.author: BM4563 (Ben Mayo)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m so surprised that you can’t filter using a range of specific dates. Seems like a massive oversight?!

posts.author: alo1 (Adeena)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes yes yes!

I need to filter by year, not just “past date” or “upcoming.”

And an option for specific dates would be a game changer

posts.author: BM4563 (Ben Mayo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Turns out you can quite easily

click on the triangle next to the filter option and you can change the condition to anything you need to search on.

It should really default to advanced filters rather than having hidden behind an inocuous triangle.

Enjoy!

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: can you provide a screenshot?

posts.author: jared (Jared Fox)
posts.cookedHtml: Yep, this is how to do it. Select Advanced Filters and it will default to starting a default filter on the “Where” line, then select your Column > Date, Select “is between”, then select your exact dates from a calendar view. Perfecto!

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
this would mean you have to constantly change these two specific dates. Instead a function like “from 5 days ago to 5 days from now” would be great.

There seams to be something similar by using the “last week/next week” filters. However they filter out everything that has a timeline which is longer than the timeframe. E.g. started 2 weeks ago but is still ongoing.

posts.author: JustAnotherPM (Stuart Hooker)
posts.cookedHtml: Try adding “current” as an additional OR statement in your filter

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: What exactly do you mean? Is there a native filter named “current”? Or do you write a function for a formular-column?

posts.author: JustAnotherPM (Stuart Hooker)
posts.cookedHtml: If you select the advanced filter option and then timeline there are a number of pre-defined settings that you can choose from e.g. Done on Time, Overdue, Current etc. I am not sure if these are specific to the subscription package you are using.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I can see the options. However the do - as so often - have trouble with subitems. But thank you

posts.author: lnuzum_climatemaster (Laura Nuzum)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this really really badly! We want to track budgetary items, but without being able to sort or search by date, it takes an act of god to figure out what we spent in a given month.

posts.author: Blumflickett (Blumflickett)
posts.cookedHtml: From using MS Access, Caspio etc I expected to find more flexible filters like “Since X days ago”. The absence of these filters means that we will not be signing up to Monday.

posts.author: daniel.lim (Daniel Lim)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just want to filter out all done items and subitems.

Please make this possible.

posts.author: CyrilB (Berthet)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding “current year” and “last year” would be great as well, we often do report on the current year and compare to last year.
